enrichment_note: Full repository scan pass: Profile is a first-class data partition within a Customer. A user is always on exactly 1 active profile at a time (User.CurrentProfileId). ProfileId is as fundamental a scoping key as CustomerId across SmsGroups, FtpSettings, ProfileApiKeys, ConversationMappings, address restrictions, positive lists, and all broadcast operations. 60+ named roles (ProfileRoleNames enum) gate capability per profile. Source verified from ServiceAlert.Core, ServiceAlert.Services, ServiceAlert.Api, ServiceAlert.DB, ServiceAlert.Test, ServiceAlert.Test.Shared.
key_insight: Profile is a subdivision of Customer. Almost everything partitioned by CustomerId is also partitioned by ProfileId. User is always on exactly 1 active profile until they switch. SmsGroups.ProfileId NOT NULL FK makes Profile the direct owner of all outbound messages.
open_contradictions: CONTRADICTION_001: Users.CurrentProfileId DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL (no FK) — 0 is 'not yet selected', bypasses all address restrictions in GetAddressRestrictionForProfile()
CONTRADICTION_002: Two WebWorkContext implementations diverge on ProfileId source (DB vs JWT claim)
CONTRADICTION_003: SmsGroups FK_SmsGroup_Profiles is NOCHECK — referential integrity not enforced at runtime
CONTRADICTION_004: No UNIQUE constraint on ProfileUserMapping(ProfileId, UserId)
